title: Dance Choreography Timeline Editor
description: The user controls Michelle's dance performance through a multi-step choreography timeline. Michelle stands on a spotlit stage wearing the Khronos sunglasses attached to her head. The user sequences dance animations, adjusts playback speed, triggers sunglasses glow effects synced to beats, and blends between animation clips to create a custom dance routine.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a dance choreography timeline editor. Load Michelle.glb and SunglassesKhronos.glb using GLTFLoader from a CDN. Attach the sunglasses model to Michelle's head bone so they follow her head movement precisely. Set up a stage environment with a dark floor plane using MeshStandardMaterial with slight reflectivity, three colored SpotLights (magenta, cyan, gold) pointing at center stage, and an AmbientLight at low intensity. Position the camera at a 3/4 angle looking at Michelle with OrbitControls enabled but constrained to prevent going below the floor. Create an AnimationMixer for Michelle and extract all available animation clips. Build an HTML overlay UI panel at the bottom of the screen containing: (1) a dropdown to select from available animation clips, (2) a Play/Pause toggle button, (3) a speed slider ranging from 0.25x to 3.0x that updates mixer timeScale, (4) a crossfade button that smoothly blends from the current animation to the selected one over 0.8 seconds using crossFadeTo, (5) a sunglasses glow toggle that adds an emissive color pulse effect to the sunglasses material cycling through RGB values using a sine wave in the render loop, (6) a spotlight color cycle button that rotates all three spotlight colors by 120 degrees on the hue wheel each press, and (7) a reset button that stops all actions, resets mixer time to zero, disables glow, and restores original spotlight colors. Validate that animation transitions only trigger when a different clip is selected than currently playing. The speed slider must clamp values and display the current multiplier. The glow effect should only animate when enabled and must restore original material properties when disabled. Ensure the render loop updates the mixer with clock delta, updates glow sine wave when active, and renders the scene. All interactions must provide visual feedback through the UI showing current state.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentAnimationIndex: number,         // index of the currently playing animation clip in the clips array
  isPlaying: boolean,         // whether the animation mixer is currently playing
  playbackSpeed: number,         // current animation playback speed multiplier (0.25 to 3.0)
  isCrossfading: boolean,         // whether a crossfade transition is currently in progress
  sunglassesGlowEnabled: boolean,         // whether the sunglasses emissive glow effect is active
  glowPhase: number,         // current phase of the sine wave for RGB glow cycling (0 to 2*PI)
  totalAnimationClips: number,         // total number of animation clips available in Michelle model
  mixerTime: number,         // current time of the animation mixer in seconds
  sunglassesAttached: boolean,         // whether sunglasses are successfully parented to head bone
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance from camera to Michelle in world units
}
